=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (11:14-11:27) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*() { ... }, ...pipeTransformations)`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowWithPipe\")"

[D2] (12:3-14:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D3] (16:14-16:40) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*() { ... }, ...pipeTransformations)`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionExpressionWithPipe\")"

[D4] (17:10-19:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D5] (22:17-22:44) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*() { ... }, ...pipeTransformations)`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionDeclarationWithPipe\")"

[D6] (23:10-25:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = Effect.fnUntraced(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.ignoreLogged);

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D1] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = Effect.fn(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.ignoreLogged);

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D1] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowWithPipe\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = Effect.fn("arrowWithPipe")(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.ignoreLogged);

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () => Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D3] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = Effect.fnUntraced(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged);

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D3] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = Effect.fn(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged);

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D3] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionExpressionWithPipe\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = Effect.fn("functionExpressionWithPipe")(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged);

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}


=== [D5] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export const functionDeclarationWithPipe = Effect.fnUntraced(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged);


=== [D5] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export const functionDeclarationWithPipe = Effect.fn(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged);


=== [D5] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionDeclarationWithPipe\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export const functionDeclarationWithPipe = Effect.fn("functionDeclarationWithPipe")(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged);


=== [D6] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_noPipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// This case differs from pipeWithSpan because the withSpan is not the last
// piped transformation, so we cannot extract the span name to use with
// Effect.fn. Instead, we should suggest Effect.fnUntraced for these cases.
// Note: unnecessaryEffectGen diagnostics ARE expected for the inner Effect.gen
// calls (each has a single-statement body). The Go test harness runs all rules,
// unlike upstream which runs only the rule matching the file name prefix.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.ignoreLogged)

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"), Effect.ignoreLogged)
}
